checks.name: Low Image Resolution
checks.description: An image doesn't have enough detail for sharp printing — effective DPI (after the page CTM scales the image) is below the configured minimum. Fires on both colour and grayscale images.

checks.name: Excessive Resolution
checks.description: An image has far more detail than needed, inflating file size and RIP processing time without improving printed output.

checks.name: Wrong Color Mode
checks.description: An image uses screen colors (RGB) instead of print colors (CMYK).

checks.name: No Image Compression
checks.description: An image isn't compressed, making the file unnecessarily large.

checks.name: Inline Image
checks.description: A small image is embedded directly in the page stream instead of as a resource.

checks.name: Upscaled Image
checks.description: An image has been scaled above 100% on the page, stretching it beyond its native pixel grid and causing visible blur on press.

checks.name: LZW Compression
checks.description: Image uses LZW compression which is prohibited in some print standards.

checks.name: JPEG2000 Format
checks.description: Image uses JPEG2000 (JPX) compression, which is not supported by every RIP and is disallowed by several profiles (PDF/X-1a, GWG-Sheet-2022). Re-export with JPEG or Flate compression.

checks.name: 16-Bit Image
checks.description: Image uses 16-bit color depth, unusual for standard print workflows.

checks.name: OPI Reference
checks.description: Image carries an OPI (Open Prepress Interface) reference to an external high-resolution version. The low-res placeholder will print unless the OPI server is reachable at RIP time. PDF/X profiles disallow OPI; replace with the actual high-res image.

checks.name: Alternate Image
checks.description: Image carries an alternate-images list — typically a low-resolution screen proxy alongside the print-resolution master. PDF/X profiles require alternates to be stripped before press.

checks.name: OPI Reference In Image Resource
checks.description: OPI reference discovered while walking page-resource image XObjects (complementary to LPDF_IMG_010, which fires from content-stream events). Same remediation: substitute the high-res master.

checks.name: Alternate Image In Page Resources
checks.description: Alternate-image reference discovered while walking page-resource XObjects (complementary to LPDF_IMG_011).

checks.name: Sheared Image
checks.description: Image has a non-orthogonal CTM applied — a shear, not a pure scale + rotate. Shears compress detail along one axis and can produce moire on press.

checks.name: Image Rotated Off-Axis
checks.description: Image is rotated at an angle that is not a multiple of 90° (non-orthogonal rotation). The RIP must resample at render time, softening edges. Pre-rotate the source image upstream.

checks.name: Flipped Image
checks.description: Image is mirrored — the CTM has a negative determinant. Often intentional, but worth confirming on press where reflections can indicate a wrong export setting.

checks.name: Extreme Scaling
checks.description: Image is scaled below 10% or above 1000% of its native pixel dimensions. At these extremes, file-size or quality is wasted — either downsample upstream (heavy upscaling) or remove the tiny-scale image entirely.

checks.name: Dangling Image XObject Reference
checks.description: An image XObject in page resources resolves to a missing object (xref slot absent, null object, or cycle-detection sentinel) — the RIP will render a blank region.

checks.name: Image XObject Missing /Subtype
checks.description: An image XObject dictionary has no /Subtype key, so the RIP can't know how to render it. The PDF is structurally malformed.

checks.name: Image XObject Has Wrong /Subtype
checks.description: An image XObject's /Subtype is neither /Image nor /Form (e.g., deprecated /PS, or a vendor-invented subtype the RIP won't recognise).

checks.name: Device-Dependent Color
checks.description: Color is specified without a profile, so output will vary by device.

checks.name: Spot Color Without Fallback
checks.description: A spot color has no process color fallback for proofing.

checks.name: Lab Color Used
checks.description: CIE Lab color used — may require special handling at the RIP.

checks.name: Ink Coverage Too High
checks.description: Total ink exceeds the maximum for this paper type, risking smearing or drying issues.

checks.name: Registration Color
checks.description: All inks at 100% — only for registration marks, never for artwork.

checks.name: No Output Intent
checks.description: No color profile is specified — colors may shift unpredictably during printing.

checks.name: Mixed Color Spaces
checks.description: Different color spaces used on the same page may cause inconsistency.

checks.name: CalRGB Color
checks.description: Calibrated RGB color used — should be converted to CMYK for print.

checks.name: CalGray Color
checks.description: Calibrated gray color used — should use DeviceGray for consistency.

checks.name: Indexed Color
checks.description: Indexed/palette color used — limited color range.

checks.name: Pattern Color Space
checks.description: Pattern-based color used — may render differently across RIPs.

checks.name: Separation Color
checks.description: Separation color space detected.

checks.name: DeviceN Color
checks.description: Multi-channel DeviceN color detected.

checks.name: Color Space Inventory
checks.description: Summary of color space types used in the document.

checks.name: ICC Profile Mismatch
checks.description: Embedded ICC profile doesn't match the output intent.

checks.name: RGB in CMYK Workflow
checks.description: RGB color found in a CMYK-targeted workflow.

checks.name: Impure Black
checks.description: Black areas use unnecessary color inks, risking visible misregistration.

checks.name: Untagged Color
checks.description: Color space used without an associated ICC profile.

checks.name: Wide Gamut Color
checks.description: Color is outside the typical print gamut.

checks.name: Overink Warning
checks.description: Multiple color operations accumulate high ink levels.

checks.name: Rich Black Text
checks.description: Text uses more than one CMYK ink — pure K (100/0/0/0) is recommended at every size to avoid misregistration.

checks.name: No ICC Profile
checks.description: No ICC color profile embedded for color-managed output.

checks.name: Invalid ICC Profile
checks.description: The embedded ICC profile is corrupt or invalid.

checks.name: ICC Version Mismatch
checks.description: ICC profile version doesn't match the PDF specification.

checks.name: Wrong ICC Device Class
checks.description: ICC profile device class doesn't match usage context.

checks.name: Output intent # has unrecognized condition
checks.description: Output intent #N has unrecognized condition 'N'

checks.name: Multiple output intents have inconsistent color space
checks.description: Multiple output intents have inconsistent color space types: N

checks.name: ICC profile
checks.description: ICC profile 'N' on page N is missing required tag(s): N

checks.name: Document uses ICC profiles with different rendering
checks.description: Document uses ICC profiles with different rendering intents: N

checks.name: ICC profile has non-D50 PCS illuminant
checks.description: ICC profile 'N' has non-D50 PCS illuminant: X=N, Y=N, Z=N (expected X=0.9642, Y=1.0, Z=0.8249)

checks.name: TAC Heatmap
checks.description: Total Area Coverage data for this page.

checks.name: Ink Separation Data
checks.description: Per-channel ink usage statistics.

checks.name: Ink Channel Inventory
checks.description: Summary of all ink channels used in the document.

checks.name: TAC Exceeds Substrate Limit
checks.description: Observed max Total Area Coverage exceeds the limit typical for the declared substrate (uncoated offset 280%, coated 300%, newsprint 240%, digital 320%, flexo 260%, gravure 300%, large-format 280%).

checks.name: Unknown Spot Color
checks.description: A spot color is used that isn't in a standard color book.

checks.name: Spot Color Fallback Issue
checks.description: The process color fallback for a spot color may not be accurate.

checks.name: Similar Spot Names
checks.description: Multiple spot colors have very similar names — may be duplicates.

checks.name: DeviceN on page colorant count
checks.description: DeviceN 'N' on page N: colorant count (N) does not match components (N)

checks.name: DeviceN on page includes process
checks.description: DeviceN 'N' on page N includes process colors: N

checks.name: Pantone spot color not found
checks.description: Pantone spot color 'N' not found in reference database — upload custom Pantone data for Delta-E validation

checks.name: Unknown spot color does not match
checks.description: Unknown spot color 'N' does not match any known color library (PANTONE, HKS, TOYO, DIC, RAL)

checks.name: Spot color library has alternate
checks.description: Spot color 'N' (N library) has alternate space 'N' which is not expected (expected: N)

checks.name: Duplicate spot color
checks.description: Duplicate spot color 'N' on page N defined N time(s) with different parameters: N

checks.name: Spot Color Count Exceeds Maximum
checks.description: The document declares more spot colours than the configured maximum allowed by the active profile. Excess spots inflate plate counts and press time — consolidate or convert to process where possible.

checks.name: Spot Color Used At 0% Tint
checks.description: A spot colour is referenced at 0% tint and contributes nothing to the rendered output, but it still allocates a separation plate. Either remove the reference or apply a non-zero tint.

checks.name: Deprecated Pantone Suffix
checks.description: Spot name uses a legacy Pantone suffix (CV, CVC, CVU, CVP, CVUX) that was retired with the post-2008 Pantone book; verify the spot still maps to the intended colour.

checks.name: Non-Canonical Spot Name
checks.description: Spot colour name doesn't match the lintPDF canonical taxonomy (CutContour, Crease, Perforation, KissCut, ThroughCut, White, Varnish, VarnishFree). Renaming to canonical names improves cross-vendor compatibility (Esko, PackZ, ArtiosCAD).

checks.name: Overprint On
checks.description: Overprint is enabled — colors will mix where they overlap instead of knocking out.

checks.name: Overprint Off for Spot
checks.description: Overprint is disabled for a spot color, which may cause unexpected knockouts.

checks.name: Overprint Mode Mismatch
checks.description: Overprint mode setting is inconsistent.

checks.name: White overprint on page fill
checks.description: White overprint on page N (fill is white with overprint active — content underneath will show through)

checks.name: Overprint Inventory
checks.description: Informational summary of every object that has overprint enabled, broken down by colour space. Useful for comparing overprint behaviour against the production proof and the operator's expectations.

checks.name: Overprint active with DeviceRGB
checks.description: Overprint active with DeviceRGB on page N (undefined behavior on press)

checks.name: Small black text instance min
checks.description: N small black text instanceN (min Npt) in knockout mode on page N (overprint not active — risk of misregistration)

checks.name: Registration color with overprint active
checks.description: Registration color with overprint active on page N (registration color outside marks is dangerous)

checks.name: Transparency Used
checks.description: Page uses transparency which must be flattened for older workflows.

checks.name: Transparency × Overprint Interaction
checks.description: Both transparency and overprint are active on the same page. The interaction between alpha-blended objects and overprinted spot colours is RIP-specific — flattening behaviour can swap colours or drop objects unpredictably. Verify on a proof.

checks.name: Soft Mask
checks.description: Image uses a soft mask, increasing rendering complexity.

checks.name: Low Opacity
checks.description: Content has very low opacity and may be nearly invisible in print.

checks.name: Transparency Group
checks.description: A transparency group is defined on this page.

checks.name: Knockout transparency group on page may
checks.description: Knockout transparency group on page N (may cause unexpected rendering)

checks.name: Shading pattern detected
checks.description: Shading pattern detected on page N (N shadingN, potential gradient banding risk)

checks.name: Transparency Blend / OutputIntent CS Mismatch
checks.description: Transparency-group blending colour space differs from the OutputIntent destination colour space. Flatteners may render the page colour-shifted relative to the printed proof.

checks.name: Transparency on Spot Page
checks.description: Page declares Separation / DeviceN spot colour spaces and also has transparency events. Some RIPs flatten transparency to process colour and lose the spot.

checks.name: Missing Font
checks.description: A font is not embedded — text may display incorrectly at the printer.

checks.name: Full Font Embedded
checks.description: The entire font is embedded instead of just the characters used, increasing file size.

checks.name: System Font Used
checks.description: A standard system font is used which may render differently on other devices.

checks.name: Type 3 Font
checks.description: User-drawn Type 3 font detected — quality may vary across RIPs.

checks.name: Missing ToUnicode
checks.description: CID font is missing a ToUnicode map — text search and copy may not work. Currently fires on CID fonts only; the broader F-13 case (ToUnicode missing on any font subtype) is partially covered here.

checks.name: Missing CIDSystemInfo
checks.description: CID font is missing system information needed for correct rendering.

checks.name: No Font Encoding
checks.description: Font lacks encoding information — characters may map incorrectly.

checks.name: TrueType Not Embedded
checks.description: A TrueType font is referenced but not embedded in the file.

checks.name: OpenType Not Embedded
checks.description: An OpenType/CFF font is referenced but not embedded.

checks.name: Incomplete Font
checks.description: Font descriptor is present but the actual font data is missing.

checks.name: Multiple Master Font
checks.description: Multiple Master font detected — limited RIP support.

checks.name: Faux Bold
checks.description: Software-simulated bold detected — the renderer is synthesising weight from a non-bold font variant rather than using a real bold weight, so glyphs print thinner and less consistently than expected. Replace with the actual bold variant or accept the design choice.

checks.name: Faux Italic
checks.description: Software-simulated italic detected — the renderer is shearing the upright glyph rather than using a true italic variant. Glyph forms are not the designer's italic intent. Replace with the actual italic variant or accept the design choice.

checks.name: Damaged Font
checks.description: The font program appears corrupt or has a type mismatch.

checks.name: Restricted Font Embedding Licence
checks.description: Font's OS/2 fsType bit advertises a licence restriction (restricted / preview-and-print / editable embedding). Verify vendor licensing before distributing this PDF.

checks.name: Font Subset Violates No-Subsetting Policy
checks.description: Font vendor set the OS/2 no_subsetting bit, but the PDF subsetted this font anyway. Disabled by default; enable for licensed-font-only workflows.

checks.name: Outline Embed Violates Bitmap-Only Policy
checks.description: Font vendor set the OS/2 bitmap_only bit, but the PDF embedded outline data. Disabled by default; enable for licensed-font-only workflows.

checks.name: Small Text
checks.description: Text is below the minimum readable size for print.

checks.name: Very small text pt effective
checks.description: Very small text (Npt effective) on page N (below Npt)

checks.name: Invisible Text (Rendering Mode 3)
checks.description: Text is rendered with mode 3 — neither filled nor stroked. Used to make text searchable but invisible (overlay on a scanned image, for instance). On press the text won't print but it still occupies the object stream and may confuse RIPs that expect every text object to render.

checks.name: White Text
checks.description: White text detected — verify it's intentional and not hidden content.

checks.name: Text Painted In Registration Colour
checks.description: Text is painted in registration colour (100% on every CMYK channel). Registration is reserved for crop marks and trapping guides — text in registration overprints heavily and bleeds when ink trapping fails. Re-tag with the intended ink.

checks.name: Small multi-ink text pt inks
checks.description: Small multi-ink text (Npt, N inks) on page N (risk of misregistration)

checks.name: Text Near Fold Line
checks.description: Text region within the configured clearance (default 3.0mm) of a fold / crease / score line. Text that crosses or hugs a fold gets bent and becomes hard to read.

checks.name: Text Overlaps Dieline Cut Path
checks.description: Text region overlaps the dieline cut path. At the cutter, glyphs will be physically sliced — the printed product reads as a typo even though the source artwork is correct. Distinct from text-near-fold (clearance) — this fires on actual intersection. Reposition the text or move the dieline before re-exporting.

checks.name: Reverse Text Minimum Stroke
checks.description: Small white (reverse / knockout) text was rendered without a stroke. Add a ≥0.5pt stroke or use ≥12pt for legibility on press.

checks.name: Text Under Soft Mask
checks.description: Text rendered on a page that declares a soft-mask ExtGState. Some RIPs lose legibility on text under a soft mask; verify rendering at production resolution.

checks.name: Hairline Stroke
checks.description: A very thin line may disappear or print inconsistently.

checks.name: Small Text on Thin Stroke
checks.description: Thin stroked text may be hard to read at small sizes.

checks.name: Hairline stroke
checks.description: Hairline stroke (Npt) on page N (below Npt minimum)

checks.name: Zero-width stroke on page will not
checks.description: Zero-width stroke on page N (will not render in print)

checks.name: Butt Cap on Thin Line
checks.description: A butt line cap on a thin stroke may cause visible gaps — round cap recommended.

checks.name: Multi-ink thin stroke pt inks
checks.description: Multi-ink thin stroke (Npt, N inks) on page N (risk of misregistration on thin lines)

checks.name: Invisible stroke white/zero-opacity on page renders
checks.description: Invisible stroke (white/zero-opacity) on page N (renders as white line art)

checks.name: Non-default flatness tolerance
checks.description: Non-default flatness tolerance (N) on page N (may affect curve rendering quality)

checks.name: Rich Black Stroke
checks.description: A thin stroke (0.5-1.0pt) uses more than one CMYK ink - pure K or a single spot is recommended on fine line work.

checks.name: Excessive path points
checks.description: Excessive path points (N) on page N (may cause RIP slowdown or failure)

checks.name: White Fill Path
checks.description: A white-filled path may knock out background content unintentionally.

checks.name: Missing Trim/Bleed Box
checks.description: Page boundaries needed for cutting and bleed are not defined. The analyzer emits one finding per missing box; the discriminator lives in `details.missing_box` (`TrimBox` or `BleedBox`).

checks.name: Box Hierarchy Violated
checks.description: Page boxes (Media, Crop, Bleed, Trim) are not properly nested per ISO 32000-2 §14.11.2. Fires on three conditions discriminated by `details.violation`: CropBox extends outside MediaBox, BleedBox extends outside CropBox, or TrimBox extends outside BleedBox.

checks.name: Insufficient Bleed
checks.description: One or more sides have less bleed allowance than the configured minimum. White edges may show after cutting if the image doesn't extend far enough past the trim edge.

checks.name: Empty Page
checks.description: Page has no visible content.

checks.name: Content In Safety Margin
checks.description: Critical content sits within the configured safety margin of the trim edge. Movement during finishing can shift this content into the cut zone — pull it inward upstream.

checks.name: Content Beyond Bleed
checks.description: Content extends outside the BleedBox. Anything past the bleed will be clipped by the RIP and is wasted file size at best, or evidence of a misconfigured export at worst.

checks.name: UserUnit Scaling Active
checks.description: Page uses a UserUnit other than 1.0 — coordinates are scaled globally. Common in large-format work but can confuse imposition and downstream measurements that assume default units.

checks.name: Non-Standard Page Rotation
checks.description: Page has a /Rotate value other than 0° (typically 90°/180°/270°). RIPs honour rotation, but imposition tools and operator workflows expect upright pages by default.

checks.name: Inconsistent Page Sizes
checks.description: Document contains pages of different sizes. Mixed-size jobs need explicit handling at imposition; verify this is intentional.

checks.name: Page Size Mismatch
checks.description: Page dimensions don't match the product size declared on the profile (`expected_page_width_mm` / `expected_page_height_mm`). Tolerance defaults to 0.5mm; either orientation is accepted.

checks.name: Multiple Page Sizes
checks.description: Document contains pages of different sizes which may complicate imposition.

checks.name: Document has inconsistent page rotations
checks.description: Document has inconsistent page rotations: N

checks.name: Document has no title in Info dictionary
checks.description: Document has no title in Info dictionary

checks.name: Document is encrypted not allowed in print
checks.description: Document is encrypted (not allowed in print workflows)

checks.name: Linearized PDF detected web-optimized may need
checks.description: Linearized PDF detected (web-optimized, may need re-saving for print)

checks.name: Incremental updates detected trailer has /Prev
checks.description: Incremental updates detected (trailer has /Prev reference, file may contain stale data)

checks.name: File size MB exceeds threshold
checks.description: File size (N MB) exceeds threshold (N MB)

checks.name: Pre-separated pages detected pages with single
checks.description: Pre-separated pages detected (N pages with single Separation color space)

checks.name: PDF Version Outside Profile Range
checks.description: The PDF header version sits outside the range the active preflight profile expects (below min_pdf_version or above max_pdf_version). Re-save with a matching compatibility setting.

checks.name: JavaScript Found
checks.description: Document contains JavaScript which is not allowed in print workflows.

checks.name: Form Fields Present
checks.description: Interactive form fields detected — these won't print as expected.

checks.name: PDF Layers Detected
checks.description: Optional content layers (OCGs) are present.

checks.name: Embedded Files
checks.description: Document contains embedded file attachments.

checks.name: 3D annotation found
checks.description: 3D annotation found on page N

checks.name: Document contains XFA forms not supported
checks.description: Document contains XFA forms (not supported in print workflows)

checks.name: Document is a tagged PDF structure tree
checks.description: Document is a tagged PDF (structure tree present)

checks.name: JavaScript action detected in page actions
checks.description: JavaScript action detected in page N actions (security and print workflow risk)

checks.name: Interactive Form Fields Present
checks.description: The document contains interactive form fields (text inputs, buttons, dropdowns, signatures). Form fields are usually stripped for press output and may indicate the wrong file was uploaded.

checks.name: Layer configuration has print-specific visibility
checks.description: Layer configuration has print-specific visibility rules for N layer(s)

checks.name: PostScript fragment Type 1 XObject detected
checks.description: PostScript fragment (Type 1 XObject) detected on page N (prohibited in modern PDF/X workflows)

checks.name: Document contains bookmarks/outlines entries
checks.description: Document contains bookmarks/outlines (N entries)

checks.name: Embedded page thumbnails detected starting
checks.description: Embedded page thumbnails detected (starting on page N — increases file size unnecessarily)

checks.name: Non-JavaScript action detected in document catalog
checks.description: Non-JavaScript action 'N' detected in document catalog (may cause unexpected behavior in print workflow)

checks.name: No XMP Metadata
checks.description: XMP metadata stream is missing — may be required by some standards.

checks.name: Title mismatch Info dict vs XMP
checks.description: Title mismatch: Info dict 'N' vs XMP 'N'

checks.name: Trapped key is in XMP metadata
checks.description: Trapped key is N in XMP metadata

checks.name: PDF version mismatch header vs XMP
checks.description: PDF version mismatch: header 'N' vs XMP 'N'

checks.name: Printable Annotation
checks.description: A printable annotation is inside the trim area and will appear in output.

checks.name: Multimedia annotation
checks.description: Multimedia annotation (N) on page N

checks.name: Link Annotation
checks.description: A clickable link annotation is present on this page.

checks.name: Stamp annotation
checks.description: Stamp annotation on page N

checks.name: Non-printing annotation in trim area
checks.description: Non-printing N annotation in trim area on page N

checks.name: TrapNet annotation on page embedded trapping
checks.description: TrapNet annotation on page N (embedded trapping may conflict with RIP trapping settings)

checks.name: Not Tagged
checks.description: Document has no structure tree — it's not accessible to screen readers.

checks.name: No Language Set
checks.description: Document language is not specified, reducing accessibility.

checks.name: Document is a tagged PDF StructTreeRoot
checks.description: Document is a tagged PDF (StructTreeRoot and MarkInfo present)

checks.name: Missing Document Language
checks.description: The /Lang entry is missing from the document catalog.

checks.name: Image on page may lack
checks.description: Image 'N' on page N may lack alternative text (/Alt)

checks.name: No heading structure found in document missing
checks.description: No heading structure found in document (missing /H1../H6 tags)

checks.name: Table structure found but no table header
checks.description: Table structure found but no table header (/TH) elements present

checks.name: List items found without proper list container
checks.description: List items found without proper list container (/L) structure

checks.name: No Artifact markings found in structure tree
checks.description: No Artifact markings found in structure tree; decorative elements may not be properly excluded

checks.name: Reading order undefined /StructTreeRoot has no /K
checks.description: Reading order undefined (/StructTreeRoot has no /K children)

checks.name: No structural emphasis tags /Em /Strong found
checks.description: No structural emphasis tags (/Em, /Strong) found; information may be conveyed by color alone

checks.name: No Output Intent for Contrast
checks.description: Cannot verify text-background contrast for accessibility without an output intent.

checks.name: Tab order not specified
checks.description: Tab order not specified on page N (/Tabs missing)

checks.name: Heading Hierarchy Skip
checks.description: Heading hierarchy skips a level (e.g., H1 → H3 without H2). Breaks screen-reader navigation and outline browsing (WCAG 1.3.1).

checks.name: Encryption Denies Screen Reader
checks.description: Encryption /P permission bit 10 is cleared — screen readers can't extract text or graphics for accessibility (ISO 32000-2 §7.6.4.2 Table 22).

checks.name: Table Header Cell Missing /Scope or /Headers
checks.description: Table /TH cell lacks /Scope or /Headers attribute — screen readers can't associate data cells with their headers (WCAG 1.3.1).

checks.name: Barcode Detected
checks.description: A potential barcode pattern was identified on this page.

checks.name: Barcode decode failed
checks.description: Barcode decode failed on page N — candidate region could not be decoded

checks.name: Barcode grade
checks.description: Barcode grade 'N' on page N is below minimum 'N'

checks.name: Barcode quiet zone
checks.description: Barcode quiet zone on page N is Nmm, below required Nmm

checks.name: Barcode symbol contrast below B grade
checks.description: Barcode symbol contrast N below B grade on page N

checks.name: Poor barcode edge contrast CV=
checks.description: Poor barcode edge contrast (CV=N) on page N

checks.name: Barcode quiet zone mm below ISO minimum
checks.description: Barcode quiet zone Nmm below ISO minimum Nmm on page N

checks.name: Barcode Bar-Width Deviation
checks.description: Measured bar widths deviate from the nominal X-dimension by more than the ISO 15416 tolerance. Excessive deviation lowers the print grade and risks scanner read failures.

checks.name: Possible barcode defect
checks.description: Possible barcode defect on page N — only N strokes detected

checks.name: Barcode modulation below C grade
checks.description: Barcode modulation N below C grade on page N

checks.name: Barcode decodability below C grade
checks.description: Barcode decodability N below C grade on page N

checks.name: Potential 2D barcode detected
checks.description: Potential 2D barcode detected on page N (N modules in NxNpt region)

checks.name: 2D barcode grid irregularity
checks.description: 2D barcode grid irregularity on page N (width CV=N, height CV=N)

checks.name: 2D barcode on page contains
checks.description: 2D barcode on page N contains N modules

checks.name: 2D barcode aspect ratio
checks.description: 2D barcode aspect ratio N on page N (expected near 1.0 for most 2D symbologies)

checks.name: 2D barcode size xmm
checks.description: 2D barcode size NxNmm on page N

checks.name: Barcode candidate on page — verify
checks.description: Barcode candidate on page N — verify GS1 format compliance if applicable

checks.name: Barcode candidate on page — verify
checks.description: Barcode candidate on page N — verify application identifier if applicable

checks.name: Barcode placement on page — center
checks.description: Barcode placement on page N — center at (N, N)pt

checks.name: Barcode symbology on page — verify
checks.description: Barcode symbology on page N — verify symbology meets specification requirements

checks.name: Barcode data length
checks.description: Barcode data length on page N — verify encoded data length meets requirements

checks.name: Barcode color compliance
checks.description: Barcode color compliance on page N — verify dark bars (K>0.7 or Gray<0.3) on light background

checks.name: Low Barcode Resolution
checks.description: The barcode area resolution is below the minimum for reliable scanning.

checks.name: Barcode in portrait orientation
checks.description: Barcode in portrait orientation on page N (may affect scanning)

checks.name: Multiple barcode candidates
checks.description: Multiple barcode candidates (N) on page N

checks.name: Barcode extends beyond trim box
checks.description: Barcode extends beyond trim box on page N (will be trimmed)

checks.name: Barcode center is mm from page center
checks.description: Barcode center is Nmm from page center on page N — may be near a fold

checks.name: Barcode height mm below ISO minimum
checks.description: Barcode height Nmm below ISO minimum on page N

checks.name: EU DPP URL Issues
checks.description: EU Digital Product Passport URL in a decoded barcode payload is not HTTPS or contains malformed characters.

checks.name: Invalid GS1 AI Syntax
checks.description: Decoded barcode payload contains a GS1 Application Identifier whose value does not satisfy its expected syntax (length / character set).

checks.name: Barcode Quiet Zone Conflict
checks.description: An image XObject (likely a barcode) sits closer than the configured quiet zone (default 2.5mm) to a dieline / fold / crease line. The cut blade or fold crease may pass through the barcode quiet zone and break scanability.

checks.name: UDI Barcode Issues
checks.description: UDI (Unique Device Identifier) barcode is missing required AIs (e.g. AI 01 GTIN) or none of the recommended production AIs (17 / 10 / 21) are present.

checks.name: Dieline Detected

checks.name: Cosmetics Label Issue

checks.name: Cosmetic Ingredient List Issue
checks.description: Cosmetic ingredient list deviates from INCI ordering or minimum-type-size rules.

checks.description: Potency or dosage labeling deviates from state cannabis requirements.

checks.description: 'N' on page N encroaches the safe zone (Nmm)

checks.description: Generated visual embedding for page N (N-dimensional, model: N)

checks.name: Page Count Changed vs Reference
checks.description: The reference document and the current document have different page counts. Verify the version compared against is correct.

checks.name: Page Differs From Reference
checks.description: A page differs from the reference version (SSIM below threshold or non-trivial pixel-change percentage). Visually inspect to confirm the change is intentional.

checks.name: Text Not Outlined
checks.description: Text has not been converted to outlines for guaranteed rendering.

checks.name: Page contains text converted to outlines
checks.description: Page N contains text converted to outlines. OCR detected N characters of visible text but only N characters are extractable as live text.

checks.name: Outlined text region
checks.description: Outlined text region on page N: 'N'

checks.name: Dieline Detected
checks.description: A dieline was detected via spot name, layer name, or AI vision fallback.

checks.name: No Dieline Detected
checks.description: Expected dieline not found in a packaging file. Verify spot naming or AI detection availability.

checks.name: No Dieline Found
checks.description: No die line detected — file does not appear to be packaging artwork.

checks.description: Organic certification mark may be missing or incorrectly placed.

checks.name: USDA Organic Seal Misuse
checks.description: USDA organic seal used on a product below the required organic-content threshold.

checks.name: EU Font Size Violation

checks.description: Only N historical submissions available (need N for SPC). Trend analysis will improve with more data.

checks.description: Text doesn't have enough contrast against its background for comfortable reading.

checks.description: QR code on page N contains invalid URL: N — 'N'

checks.name: DataMatrix on page GS1 AI
checks.description: DataMatrix on page N: GS1 AI (N) has invalid value 'N'

checks.name: Decoded DataMatrix barcode
checks.description: Decoded DataMatrix barcode on page N: 'N'

checks.name: Barcode on page X-dimension
checks.description: N barcode on page N: X-dimension N mm is below absolute minimum N mm (80% magnification)

checks.name: Barcode on page magnification
checks.description: N barcode on page N: magnification N is below absolute minimum N

checks.name: Barcode on page height
checks.description: N barcode on page N: height N mm is critically below minimum N mm

checks.name: Barcode on page right quiet zone
checks.description: N barcode on page N: right quiet zone ~N modules, minimum N required

checks.name: Braille Zone Integrity
checks.description: Pharma-packaging Braille spot detected. Severity escalates to warning when other inks paint inside the Braille zone (dots fill in and become unreadable).

checks.name: Dieline Spot Used As Fill
checks.description: Dieline spot colour is applied as a fill, not just a stroke. The cutter will follow the filled region as a closed path. Change the fill to the intended print ink (common Canva-export bug).

checks.name: Dieline Has Non-Normal Blend Mode
checks.description: Dieline spot is painted with a blend mode other than Normal (Multiply, Darken, etc.). Cutter spots are layer-extracted process controls, not artwork — non-Normal blend modes are silently dropped or composited by the RIP, leaving the cut plate missing or in the wrong colour. Set blend mode to Normal on the dieline layer.

checks.name: Content Outside Dieline
checks.description: Paint bbox extends beyond the dieline polygon envelope by more than the configured tolerance. Content may be clipped or trimmed in production.

checks.name: Excessive Bleed Past Dieline
checks.description: Artwork extends past the dieline polygon by more than the configured max_bleed_mm. Excessive bleed wastes paper and may complicate multi-up imposition.

checks.name: Dieline Set To Knockout
checks.description: Dieline stroke is set to knockout (OP=false) — underlying inks will have gaps along cut lines. Enable 'Overprint Stroke' on the dieline layer in Illustrator / InDesign.

checks.name: Foreign Content On Dieline Layer
checks.description: Non-dieline paint operation(s) found inside a dieline-named OCG marked-content block. Artwork on the cutter plate will print on every copy — move it to a non-dieline layer.

checks.name: Dieline Missing
checks.description: Expected dieline not present in a packaging file. Neither name-match nor vision fallback detected a cut contour.

checks.name: Dieline Multi-Colour
checks.description: Dieline layer / spot contains multiple stroke colours — a clean cut path should be exactly one ink.

checks.name: Dieline Has Reduced Opacity
checks.description: Dieline spot is painted with alpha < 100% (semi-transparent). Cutter spots are layer-extracted process controls, not artwork — partial transparency does not survive RIP separation, so the cut plate either drops to zero coverage or composites into the substrate. Set opacity to 100% on the dieline layer in Illustrator / InDesign.

checks.name: Dieline Feature Below Cutter Resolution
checks.description: Dieline polygon's bbox or perimeter is smaller than the cutting machine can track cleanly (default 1.0mm threshold). Tiny features tear surrounding stock or crumble during die-cutting.

checks.name: Varnish Applied Inside VarnishFree Region
checks.description: Varnish / coating spot overlaps a VarnishFree (no-coating) region. Remove varnish from the marked region in the source file before exporting.

checks.name: White Underprint Gap
checks.description: White / OpaqueWhite underprint covers less than the configured fraction of the dieline area (default 95%). On clear or foil substrates, gaps in white underprint let the substrate show through colour artwork.

checks.name: Dieline Below Artwork
checks.description: Dieline is painted before artwork in the content stream — the cutter marker should sit on top of all content. Move the dieline layer to the top of the layer stack before exporting.

checks.name: Digimarc Watermark Hint
checks.description: XMP metadata contains a Digimarc namespace or URL token. The actual watermark requires the licensed Digimarc SDK to verify.

checks.name: ECG Readiness Assessment
checks.description: Reports whether the document is structured for Expanded Colour Gamut (CMYKOGV) reproduction: spot-colour count, presence of CMYKOGV-like DeviceN spaces, and overall ECG-appropriateness.

checks.name: ECG achievability Spot color could not
checks.description: ECG achievability: Spot color 'N' could not be mapped to a Lab value for gamut testing (found on page(s) N). Provide an ICC profile or…

checks.name: ECG TAC Exceeds Limit
checks.description: Total Area Coverage on a multi-channel (DeviceN) ECG separation exceeds the configured limit. ECG presses tolerate higher TAC than CMYK but still have a substrate-specific ceiling — exceeding it causes drying, finishing, and trapping problems.

checks.name: DeviceN 7-colorant space
checks.description: DeviceN 7-colorant space 'N' on page N has inconsistent naming: N. NN

checks.name: ECG Ink-Build Exceeds 3-Ink Maximum
checks.description: More than three inks are active simultaneously above the significance threshold. ECG prefers a maximum 3-ink build for colour stability — additional inks create gray-balance drift and compound dot-gain.

checks.name: Spot color could be replaced
checks.description: Spot color 'N' could be replaced by ECG process color 'N' on page(s) N

checks.name: ECG color out of build range CMYK+OGV
checks.description: ECG color out of build range: CMYK+OGV TAC N% exceeds maximum allowable 400% on page N

checks.name: ECG Gray-Balance Drift Risk
checks.description: Objects use a near-equal C / M / Y mix above the high-ink threshold. In ECG workflows these neutrals depend on cross-press consistency that single-pass orange / green / violet inks rarely deliver — colour shift is likely. Convert critical neutrals to a K-anchored recipe.

checks.name: ECG Overinking
checks.description: Total Area Coverage exceeds the ECG-specific threshold. Excess ink on an ECG press produces drying problems, ink trapping issues, and back-of-sheet contamination on multi-pass jobs.

checks.name: Missing ECG characterization data no ECG-specific
checks.description: Missing ECG characterization data: no ECG-specific output intent or characterization reference (e.g., FOGRA55) found in document metadata

checks.name: ECG ink channel limit exceeded channel(s
checks.description: ECG ink channel limit exceeded: channel(s) N above N% on page N

checks.name: Gamut boundary mapping required spot color
checks.description: Gamut boundary mapping required: spot color 'N' on page(s) N may be out of ECG gamut and would need gamut mapping for accurate reproduction

checks.name: ECG Small Text Built From Multiple Inks
checks.description: Small text below the legibility threshold is built from multiple inks instead of K-only. Multi-ink small text is fragile to registration error on ECG presses — convert to K-only or enlarge the type.

checks.name: ECG Non-Standard Rich-Black Recipe
checks.description: Objects use a rich-black recipe outside the configured ECG default (typically C=60, M=40, Y=40, K=100). Non-standard recipes may shift colour or produce inconsistent neutrals across press runs.

checks.name: ECG trap zone warning path element(s
checks.description: ECG trap zone warning: N path element(s) with stroke width below Npt across N page(s) may need special trapping in ECG workflow

checks.name: ECG ICC profile version
checks.description: ECG ICC profile version N is below v4.0; ECG workflows require ICC v4 or later for accurate multicolor profiling

checks.name: DeviceN colorant order
checks.description: DeviceN colorant order N on page N does not follow CMYKOGV convention N

checks.name: ECG high ink per channel channel(s
checks.description: ECG high ink per channel: channel(s) N exceed N% on page N

checks.name: EPM K-Channel Usage
checks.description: Objects use the K (black) channel. In HP Indigo EPM (Enhanced Productivity Mode) only C, M, Y print, so K-bearing objects either drop to white or fall back to a CMY composite — neither is what the designer asked for. Move pure-black to a CMY composite, or route the job to standard CMYK instead of EPM.

checks.name: EPM Pure-Black Text
checks.description: Text uses pure K-only or DeviceGray and will not print in EPM mode (C, M, Y only). Convert pure black text to a CMY composite black before press, or route the job to CMYK rather than EPM.

checks.name: EPM Weak CMY Composite Black
checks.description: Objects with K=0 and C+M+Y under 200% will produce a weak, washed-out black on press in CMY-only mode. Good density requires roughly C≥80%, M≥70%, Y≥70%.

checks.name: EPM CMY TAC Exceeds Threshold
checks.description: Maximum CMY-only Total Area Coverage (K excluded) exceeds the configured EPM threshold. Excess CMY ink overprints, smears, and slows press throughput. Either reduce coverage upstream or route the job to standard CMYK.

checks.name: EPM Spot Color K-Dependent Fallback
checks.description: A spot color's CMYK alternate values include the K channel. When the spot is unavailable on press and the RIP falls back to alternate values, EPM mode strips K and shifts the rendered colour. Re-author the spot's alternate values to a CMY-only mix.

checks.name: EPM Image K-Channel Dependency
checks.description: CMYK image(s) carry significant K-channel data. In EPM mode the K plate is dropped, so images either lose density or are silently re-separated by the RIP. Re-export images as CMY composite or route to CMYK.

checks.name: EPM Registration Color In Artwork
checks.description: Objects are painted in registration colour (all CMYK ≥ ~90%) — a marks-and-bleeds-only ink. In EPM mode the K component is dropped and the object reads as 100% C+M+Y. Replace registration with the intended process or spot ink.

checks.name: EPM Gray Balance Risk
checks.description: Objects use a neutral CMY mix (C ≈ M ≈ Y, all above the gray-balance threshold) with K=0. Without K to anchor the neutral, EPM-mode presses are at high risk of a colour shift. Convert critical neutrals to a recipe that includes K, or route to CMYK.

checks.name: EPM Toner Limit Exceeded
checks.description: Total toner area coverage (C + M + Y + K) exceeds the configured EPM device limit (default 280%). Excess toner causes drying, finishing, and registration problems on digital presses.

checks.name: EPM Per-Channel Ink Limit
checks.description: One or more individual ink channels exceeds 95% coverage. Even when total TAC is acceptable, single-channel saturation can exceed substrate-specific limits on digital presses (especially synthetic and uncoated stocks).

checks.name: EPM Spot Color Fidelity Risk
checks.description: Spot colour may not reproduce accurately on digital devices. Verify the proof on the target press, or convert to a process build with documented gamut coverage.

checks.name: EPM Variable-Data Indicators
checks.description: Document carries variable-data indicators (PDF/VT MarkInfo or associated files). EPM throughput gains may not apply to VDP runs; check press configuration before scheduling.

checks.name: EPM Custom Halftone In ExtGState
checks.description: ExtGState carries a custom halftone dictionary. Digital presses use their own internal screening; custom halftones are typically ignored and may flag a workflow incompatibility.

checks.name: EPM Output Intent Profile-Class Mismatch
checks.description: Output Intent ICC profile class is not 'prtr' (printer) or 'mntr' (monitor) — digital presses need a device-specific output profile to render correctly. Re-tag the document with the press's calibrated ICC.

checks.name: EPM White-Ink Underlayer Detected
checks.description: A Separation color space resembling white ink was detected. White underlayers are used for dark substrate printing and are typically not part of an EPM workflow — confirm the press supports the white-ink station before proceeding.

checks.name: EPM Overprint Simulation Mode
checks.description: ExtGState has overprint enabled (OP=true) with a non-default OPM. Digital presses simulate rather than physically overprint, so the rendered result depends on RIP behaviour — verify on a proof.

checks.name: EPM High Object Count
checks.description: A page has more drawn objects than the advisory threshold (default 5,000). High object counts can slow digital-press RIP processing — consider flattening, simplifying, or rasterising heavy regions upstream.

checks.name: EPM Thin Stroke Below Digital Press Threshold
checks.description: Stroked path(s) have line width below the configured digital-press minimum (default 0.35pt). Thin strokes may break or print inconsistently on digital substrates.

checks.name: CMYK color
checks.description: CMYK color (N, N, N, N) is out of gamut for N (Lab N, N, N, distance N, N conversion)

checks.name: Target gamut volume for Lab^3
checks.description: Target gamut volume for N: N Lab^3 units

checks.name: Gamut summary for / colors out
checks.description: Gamut summary for N: N/N colors out of gamut (N RGB, N CMYK)

checks.name: Grain Direction Missing
checks.description: XMP metadata carries no grain-direction key. Downstream press / finishing operations may not get the substrate orientation from this PDF.

checks.name: Page BleedBox Extends Past Dieline
checks.description: The page's declared /BleedBox extends past the dieline polygon envelope. The press-side bleed allowance does not fit within the cutter region — imposition will waste paper or clip adjacent units on a multi-up sheet. Either tighten the BleedBox in the export profile or expand the dieline so the bleed sits inside it.

checks.name: PDF/A Non-Conformant
checks.description: veraPDF reported one or more PDF/A rule failures (1b / 2b / 2u / 3b / 3u / 4). See details.failures for the clause + test-number list.

checks.name: PDF/VT Structural Issue
checks.description: Document declares PDF/VT (variable-data printing per ISO 16612-2) but lacks the required structural elements such as /Catalog /DPartRoot.

checks.name: PDF/X Non-Conformant
checks.description: veraPDF reported one or more PDF/X rule failures (X-1a / X-3 / X-4 / X-6). See details.failures for the clause + test-number list.

checks.name: Custom halftone dictionary detected
checks.description: Custom halftone dictionary detected on page N

checks.name: Transfer function detected on page prohibited
checks.description: Transfer function detected on page N (prohibited in PDF/X)

checks.name: Custom BG/UCR function detected
checks.description: Custom BG/UCR function detected on page N

checks.name: Custom halftone HalftoneType detected in ExtGState
checks.description: Custom halftone (HalftoneType N) detected in ExtGState 'N' on page N

checks.name: Custom transfer curve detected in ExtGState
checks.description: Custom transfer curve (N) detected in ExtGState 'N' on page N

checks.name: Processing step layers detected
checks.description: Processing step layers detected: N

checks.name: White ink layer detected
checks.description: White ink layer detected: N

checks.name: ISO 19593-1 Positions Suggestion
checks.description: Spot ink whose name suggests a positioning aid (registration / trim mark / colour bar) should be tagged under the ISO 19593-1 'Positions' ProcessingSteps group so prepress strips it before plating.

checks.name: ISO 19593-1 Processing Step Suggestion
checks.description: Spot ink with a recognised production-process name (cut, crease, perforation, varnish, white) should be tagged with the matching ISO 19593-1 ProcessingSteps group so finishing equipment treats it as a process rather than a printable ink.

checks.name: ISO 19593-1 White Subtype Suggestion
checks.description: White spot whose name carries a hint (Underprint / Overprint / Print / Knockout) should be tagged with the matching ISO 19593-1 White subtype.

checks.name: Pharma DataMatrix on page is missing
checks.description: Pharma DataMatrix on page N is missing required EU FMD fields: N

checks.name: Pharma DataMatrix on page GTIN
checks.description: Pharma DataMatrix on page N: GTIN N has invalid check digit (expected N, got N)

checks.name: Pharma DataMatrix on page invalid expiry
checks.description: Pharma DataMatrix on page N: invalid expiry date format 'N' (expected YYMMDD)

checks.name: Pharma DataMatrix on page all EU
checks.description: Pharma DataMatrix on page N: all EU FMD fields present — GTIN N, Serial N, Batch N, Expiry N

checks.name: No DataMatrix barcode found — EU FMD
checks.description: No DataMatrix barcode found — EU FMD requires a GS1 DataMatrix (ECC200) on pharmaceutical packaging

checks.name: Conflicting 2D Barcodes Alongside Pharma DataMatrix
checks.description: Non-DataMatrix 2D barcode(s) (QR, Aztec, etc.) appear on the same page as a pharma GS1 DataMatrix. Scanners may pick the wrong symbol and cause traceability failures — remove or relocate the secondary barcodes.

checks.name: No human-readable text detected near QR code
checks.description: No human-readable text detected near QR code on page N

checks.name: QR code on page human-readable text
checks.description: QR code on page N: human-readable text matches decoded data (similarity N)

checks.name: QR code on page human-readable text
checks.description: QR code on page N: human-readable text does not match decoded data (similarity N). QR contains 'NN', nearby text: 'NN'

checks.name: QR code on page has insufficient
checks.description: QR code on page N has insufficient left quiet zone: N modules (minimum N per ISO 18004)

checks.name: QR code on page module size
checks.description: QR code on page N: module size N mm (N px at N DPI)

checks.name: QR code on page contains GS1
checks.description: QR code on page N contains GS1 Digital Link with GTIN N

checks.name: Duplicate QR code found on pages
checks.description: Duplicate QR code found on pages N: 'NN'

checks.name: Tobacco Warning Area Below Threshold
checks.description: Tobacco / cigarette artwork's health warning covers less than the regulator-required fraction of the page surface (EU TPD2 65%, US FDA 50%, AU/NZ 75%).

checks.name: PDF/UA Non-Conformant
checks.description: veraPDF Matterhorn reported one or more PDF/UA-1 checkpoint failures (accessibility). See details.failures for the list.

checks.name: Viewer DisplayDocTitle
checks.description: Catalog /ViewerPreferences /DisplayDocTitle should be true so PDF readers show the document's metadata title rather than its filename. Required by WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.2 when the title is the meaningful identifier.

checks.name: GWG Audit Trail Missing
checks.description: No GWG audit-trail namespace was found in XMP metadata. The PDF has not been through a Ghent Workgroup-aware preflight tool.
